prompt:
Anatomical muscle-activation diagram of the "弹力带踝外翻" (banded ankle eversion) exercise, front view, focused on lower leg and foot. Person posture: Athletic person seated on the floor. The right leg is extended STRAIGHT FORWARD with the heel resting on the floor. The left leg is bent with the LEFT FOOT tucked back BEHIND the buttocks (out of the way), so the LEFT LEG DOES NOT BLOCK the path between the anchor and the right foot. A loop resistance band is attached to a low floor anchor on the LEFT/MEDIAL side, positioned in front of the body and to the inside of the right foot (on the big-toe side). The band runs in a STRAIGHT, UNOBSTRUCTED LINE through OPEN AIR from the anchor on the left to the ball of the right foot — the band path is entirely OUTSIDE the body. The right foot is rotated OUTWARD (ankle eversion): the SOLE of the right foot is visibly TURNED to face the FAR RIGHT, AWAY from the body's midline. The INNER (BIG-TOE) edge of the right foot is TILTED HIGH UP, the OUTER (PINKY-TOE) edge is rolled DOWN toward the floor. View: front-three-quarter angle so both the band path AND the foot rotation are clearly visible. Plain black athletic shorts and gray tank top. CRITICAL: the resistance band must NEVER pass through, cross, or overlap with any body part — it travels through empty space from anchor to foot.. Body rendered with semi-transparent skin so underlying muscles are visible. Highlight in saturated red the primary working muscles: peroneal muscles (peroneus longus and brevis, outer side of the lower leg). Highlight in orange the secondary stabilizers: tibialis anterior and small intrinsic foot muscles. Other muscles in pale neutral gray. Add small clean labels with thin black arrow leader lines pointing from each label to its target muscle group. The labels MUST be in simplified Chinese characters using a clean sans-serif font. Use exactly the following labels: "腓骨长短肌" pointing to the peroneal muscles (outer lower leg); "胫骨前肌" pointing to the tibialis anterior (assisting); "足部稳定肌" pointing to the foot stabilizers. 

CRITICAL CONSTRAINTS — these are non-negotiable:
1. ALL TEXT IN THE IMAGE MUST BE SIMPLIFIED CHINESE CHARACTERS ONLY.
2. ABSOLUTELY NO ENGLISH WORDS, NO LATIN LETTERS, NO PINYIN ANYWHERE IN THE IMAGE.
3. NO romanization or transliteration of muscle names.
4. NO captions, NO subtitles, NO step-by-step text overlays.
5. ONLY the muscle labels listed above should appear as text.

Style: clean medical textbook illustration, off-white background, no facial features (head shape may be shown but no eyes/nose/mouth), anatomically accurate proportions, no shadows on background, no watermarks, no signature.
